title: QuantumPerformanceRecord
description: QPR v0.2.0 — a self-contained, reproducible record of one benchmark execution against one quantum device or simulator. A QPR carries everything needed to re-run the benchmark bit-for-bit and to independently verify the reported metrics.

qpr_version.description: Semantic version of the QPR schema this record conforms to. Consumers MUST reject records whose major version they do not understand.

record_id.description: Globally unique identifier (UUID v4) of this record.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp at which this record was assembled (RFC 3339).

circuits.description: Every circuit executed for this benchmark, in submission order, with content hashes for verification.

Benchmark.description: Identity and parameterization of the benchmark that produced this record.

suite_version.description: Semantic version of the benchmark implementation. Bumped whenever the circuit family, sampling procedure, or estimator changes.

parameters.description: Complete benchmark-specific parameter set (e.g. sequence lengths, qubit pairs, number of random circuits). Together with execution.seed this MUST fully determine the generated circuits.

Provider.description: The cloud provider / SDK path through which the device was reached.

region.description: Provider region or endpoint identifier, when applicable.

Device.description: The target QPU or simulator, including the calibration snapshot in effect at execution time.

version.description: Device/backend version string as reported by the provider.

num_qubits.description: Number of physical qubits available on the device.

simulator.description: True if the target is a classical simulator rather than physical hardware.

basis_gates.description: Native gate set of the device, if reported.

coupling_map.description: Directed qubit connectivity as [control, target] pairs, if reported.

calibration_snapshot_at.description: UTC timestamp of the provider calibration data captured below (RFC 3339).

calibration_snapshot.description: Raw provider calibration data (T1/T2, gate/readout errors, ...) as reported at execution time. Free-form because formats differ per provider; recorded verbatim for auditability.

Execution.description: Everything about how the circuits were executed: seed, shots, transpilation, timing. Together with benchmark.parameters this makes the run reproducible.

seed.description: Master PRNG seed used for circuit generation and sampling. Required: without it the run is not reproducible.

live.description: True if executed on paid/live hardware, false for local simulators.

submitted_at.description: UTC timestamp when the job batch was submitted (RFC 3339).

completed_at.description: UTC timestamp when all results were retrieved (RFC 3339).

job_ids.description: Provider job identifiers, for cross-referencing against provider records.

Transpilation.description: Exact transpiler configuration. Identical circuit families are submitted to every provider; only this recorded transpilation step may differ, so it must be fully captured.

sdk_version.description: Exact version of the transpiling SDK.

optimization_level.description: SDK optimization level, when the SDK uses that concept.

settings.description: Complete transpiler settings (initial layout, routing method, scheduling, ...). Free-form per SDK, recorded verbatim.

Circuit.description: One executed circuit: the abstract (pre-transpilation) definition, the transpiled form actually submitted, and SHA-256 hashes of both for verification.

index.description: Zero-based position of this circuit in the submission batch. Raw results refer to circuits by this index.

qasm3.description: OpenQASM 3 source of the abstract circuit, before transpilation.

qasm3_sha256.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the UTF-8 encoded qasm3 field.

transpiled_qasm3.description: OpenQASM 3 source of the circuit as actually submitted, after transpilation.

transpiled_qasm3_sha256.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the UTF-8 encoded transpiled_qasm3 field. Required whenever transpiled_qasm3 is present.

metadata.description: Benchmark-specific circuit metadata (e.g. sequence length, target qubits).

Results.description: Raw measurement outcomes plus derived metrics. Raw counts are always retained so metrics can be independently re-derived.

metrics.description: Derived metrics. Every metric MUST carry sample size and a confidence interval — point estimates without error bars are not valid QPR metrics.

analysis.description: Benchmark-specific intermediate analysis artifacts (e.g. per-sequence-length survival probabilities, fit residuals).

shots.description: Shots actually executed for this circuit.

counts.description: Map from measured bitstring (MSB-first, e.g. '0101') to occurrence count.

Metric.description: One derived metric with mandatory uncertainty quantification.

unit.description: Unit of the value, when applicable, e.g. 'probability', 'circuits/s'.

qubits.description: Physical qubit indices this metric pertains to, when qubit-scoped.

MetricStatistics.description: Mandatory uncertainty quantification for a metric.

sample_size.description: Number of independent samples the estimate is based on.

confidence_level.description: Confidence level of the interval, e.g. 0.95.

ci_lower.description: Lower bound of the confidence interval.

ci_upper.description: Upper bound of the confidence interval.

std_error.description: Standard error of the estimate, when defined.

estimator.description: How the estimate and interval were obtained, e.g. 'exponential_fit_bootstrap', 'binomial_wilson', 'mean_normal'.

Provenance.description: Software environment that produced this record, pinned to exact versions.

veriqant_bench_version.description: Exact veriqant-bench version that produced this record.

sdk_versions.description: Exact versions of every quantum SDK involved, e.g. {"qiskit": "1.4.2"}.

Integrity.description: Tamper-evidence for the record. content_sha256 is the SHA-256 of the canonical JSON serialization of this record with the entire 'integrity' member removed (see QPR-SPEC §Canonicalization). The optional signature signs that hash.

content_sha256.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 over the canonical JSON of the record without its 'integrity' member.

public_key.description: Base64-encoded public key of the signer.

value.description: Base64-encoded signature over the ASCII hex content_sha256.

MetricQuality.description: Estimator self-assessment. Present whenever the producing benchmark runs quality diagnostics; a metric whose fit or estimator failed its quality thresholds MUST be published with reliable=false and the reasons listed, never as a clean-looking number.

reliable.description: False when the estimate failed the benchmark's quality thresholds.
